title: Measure and improve tool metadata and naming
goal: Audit public tool names and descriptions against real operator intents, add explicit application and exclusion cues, and use a reproducible golden-prompt evaluation to justify metadata or naming changes instead of relying on intuition.

acceptance.assertion: A generated inventory records each public name, description, category, risk, effect, closest alternatives and missing application or exclusion cues.
A versioned dataset contains representative positive, negative and ambiguous intents for status, filesystem, Git, GitHub, tasks, workspaces, secrets, process control and deployment.

The evaluation reports top-choice precision, target recall, dangerous over-selection, unnecessary generic fallback and confusion pairs with a fixed model and connector snapshot identity where observable.
Explicit risk names such as destroy and secret_reveal remain prominent unless a measured replacement improves selection without hiding effect or authority.
Ambiguous effect-level names are replaced or supplemented by intent-shaped entry points only when the evaluation shows a material benefit and a compatibility path exists.
Descriptions state when to use the tool, its decisive precondition or effect, and one important exclusion when confusion is plausible, within a bounded size.
Accepted changes include before and after results on the same dataset and explicitly separate metadata effects from deferred tool loading or connector transport effects.
